title: Browser back-button after successful login re-exposes the login form and allows resubmission without re-authentication challenge
description: After a successful login and redirect to the authenticated dashboard, pressing the browser Back button may restore the login form from the browser's page cache (bfcache) in a pre-filled state. If the form can be resubmitted from the cached state, it may issue a second authentication request on an already-active session, potentially creating a second parallel session or silently succeeding. Additionally, the already-authenticated user should be redirected away from the login page rather than shown it.
expected_result: PASS: After pressing Back, the application detects the active session and either (a) immediately redirects the user to the dashboard without rendering the login form, or (b) renders the login form but the submit button is inert and no second session is created. The password field value is NOT visible in the bfcache-restored form. FAIL: (a) The login page is shown to an already-authenticated user with no redirect — information disclosure and UX confusion; (b) The cached form is resubmitted successfully, creating a second parallel session — session duplication that can cause audit log inconsistencies, billing double-charges, or bypass of concurrent-session limits; (c) The password value is visible in plain text after Back navigation due to bfcache restoring the pre-mask DOM state.

test_steps.action: Fill in the email field with a valid account email

test_steps.expected: Credentials are submitted; redirect to the authenticated dashboard begins

test_steps.action: Wait for the authenticated dashboard to fully load
test_steps.expected: Dashboard page is loaded and session is active; URL is no longer /login

test_steps.action: Press the browser Back button to navigate back to the login page
test_steps.expected: Browser navigates back; bfcache may restore the login page with previously entered values

test_steps.action: Wait to observe whether the application detects the active session and redirects away from the login page
test_steps.expected: Because the user is already authenticated, the application should detect the active session and redirect back to the dashboard — the login form should NOT remain visible to an already-authenticated user

test_steps.action: If the login form IS visible (bfcache restored it), attempt to click submit with the cached form values
test_steps.expected: If the form is submittable from cache, the application must not create a second session or expose sensitive session tokens in a duplicate response; ideally the submit is blocked or results in a redirect to dashboard

test_steps.action: Verify only one active session exists for this user account after the back-button interaction
test_steps.expected: Exactly one session is active; user is on the dashboard; no duplicate session tokens issued
